subject: docs(session): rewrite SESSION_NOTES.md as compact snapshot
body:
Audit finding #14 — SESSION_NOTES.md had grown to 624 lines, mostly
historical entries from the round-3 custom-CSS sweep, the
post-migration audit pass, the vanilla-DaisyUI 8-phase migration, and
the zero-arbitrary-values sweep. Per CLAUDE.md the doc is supposed to
be a "compact context snapshot for session continuity" — rewritten at
session end.

Replaced with a 70-line snapshot that captures only what the next
session needs:
  - Current branch + current state (1-paragraph dashboard status)
  - Vanilla-DaisyUI policy invariants (zero custom classes, documented
    exceptions list)
  - Testing posture (Playwright removed, single tripwire active)
  - Last-session work summary with commit shas (1 line each)
  - Open items requiring next-session action (aggregate.js
    investigation, live browser spot-check, TODO.md pointer)
  - Doc pointers for deeper context

The 554 lines of historical narrative removed all duplicated content
that's already in HISTORY.md and the git log — keeping it in
SESSION_NOTES inflated the file and made finding the actually-current
state harder. New entries should follow the same compact structure:
the next agent reads SESSION_NOTES first, so it should give them
exactly the context to start work, not a re-tellling of what
HISTORY.md already documents.

Tags: documentation, cleanup, audit
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: vanilla-daisyui-cleanup
Semver: patch
title: rewrite SESSION_NOTES.md as compact snapshot
